perturbation.original_text: Developer is undertaking the services set forth in this Agreement as an independent contractor, working at Developer's own hours and using Developer's own equipment and at Developer's own chosen place of work, with discretion concerning the revision of Services and Deliverables within Client's general direction.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer is undertaking the services set forth in this Agreement as an independent contractor, but will adhere to the Client's standard working hours and use the Client's equipment when on-site. The Developer will work under the Client's direct supervision and adhere to the Client's detailed instructions regarding the revision of Services and Deliverables.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the Developer as an independent contractor with autonomy over work hours, equipment, and methods. The modified text imposes client control over these aspects, blurring the line between independent contractor and employee.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 26 U.S. Code § 3121 - Definitions (relating to employment taxes)
perturbation.law_explanation: By specifying that the developer must adhere to the client's working hours, use the client's equipment, and work under direct supervision, the modified text contradicts the definition of an independent contractor under 26 U.S. Code § 3121. This section defines employment for the purposes of employment taxes, and the degree of control exercised by the client now suggests an employer-employee relationship, potentially triggering employment tax obligations that are inconsistent with the contract's assertion of independent contractor status.

Definitions (a) Wages For purposes of this chapter, the term "wages" means all remuneration for employment, including the cash value of all remuneration (including benefits) paid in any medium other than cash; except that such term shall not include- (1) in the case of the taxes imposed by sections 3101(a) and 3111(a) that part of th
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a legal contradiction because it redefines the relationship between the developer and the client in a way that undermines the original assertion of an independent contractor status. The original text emphasizes the developer's autonomy, while the changed text gives the client direct control over the developer's working hours, equipment, and work methods. According to the Law Explanation provided, these changes suggest an employer-employee relationship, potentially triggering employment tax obligations that are inconsistent with the contract's assertion of independent contractor status. 26 USC 3121 defines wages as remuneration for employment. If the developer is now effectively an employee due to the client's control, as described in the changed text and the Law Explanation, then this remuneration should be considered wages under 26 USC 3121, contradicting the stated intention of an independent contractor relationship.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Washington without regards for its conflict of laws principle. The Parties will conduct friendly negotiations to resolve any dispute arising from this Agreement, including mediation if requested by either Party. Should mediation fail, each party consents to the personal jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in King County, Washington.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Washington without regards for its conflict of laws principle. Any dispute arising from this Agreement will be resolved through binding arbitration, with each party bearing its own legal costs. The decision of the arbitrator will be final and not subject to appeal, and the Parties waive their right to a trial by jury.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies dispute resolution through negotiation, mediation, and litigation in King County, Washington courts. The modified text mandates binding arbitration, eliminating the option of court proceedings and imposing a cost structure that differs from standard litigation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Revised Code of Washington (RCW) 7.04A.060 - Waiver of right to judicial relief.
perturbation.law_explanation: By making arbitration binding and waiving the right to a jury trial without explicitly highlighting these changes, the revised text may run afoul of RCW 7.04A.060, which governs arbitration agreements in Washington State. Under this law, waivers of rights must be clear and conspicuous. If the waiver of the right to judicial relief is not sufficiently prominent or understood, the enforceability of the arbitration clause could be challenged, as a party may unknowingly be giving up their right to pursue a case in court.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: RCW 7.04A.060: Validity of agreement to arbitrate. Print RCWs > Title 7 > Chapter 7.04A > Section 7.04A.060 7.04A.050 << 7.04A.060 >> 7.04A.070 PDF RCW 7.04A.060 Validity of agreement to arbitrate. (1) An agreement contained in a record to submit to arbitration any existing or subsequent controversy arising between the parties to the agreement is valid, enforceable, and irrevocable except upon a ground that exists at law or in equity for the revocation of contract. (2) The court shall decide whether an agreement to arbitrate exists or a controversy is subject to an agreement to arbitrate. (3) An arbitrator shall decide whether a condition precedent to arbitrability has been fulfilled and whether a contract containing a valid agreement to arbitrate is enforceable. (4) If a party to a judicial proceeding challenges the existence of, or claims that a controversy is not subject to, an agreement to arbitrate, the arbitration proceeding may continue pending final resolution of the issue by the court, unless the court otherwise orders. [ 2005 c 433 s 6 .]

perturbation.justification: The original contract language allows for dispute resolution through negotiation, mediation, and litigation in court. The changed text mandates binding arbitration, which eliminates the option of court proceedings. The Law Explanation states that making arbitration binding and waiving the right to a jury trial without explicitly highlighting these changes may violate RCW 7.04A.060, which governs arbitration agreements in Washington State, because waivers of rights must be clear and conspicuous. Scraped Law Snippet 1, RCW 7.04A.060, addresses the validity of an agreement to arbitrate, stating that such agreements are valid and enforceable except upon a ground that exists at law or in equity for the revocation of contract. The change from litigation to binding arbitration, without ensuring the waiver is clear and conspicuous, contradicts the legal obligation for valid waivers, potentially making the agreement unenforceable under RCW 7.04A.060.
